data.title: DZ Bank in Germany gets MiCA license for ‘meinKrypto’ platform, offering retail access to Bitcoin, Ether, Litecoin, and Cardano.
data.description: Cooperative banks are set to offer retail access to a selection of cryptocurrencies. Initially, customers will have access to Bitcoin, Ether, Litecoin, and Cardano. This move aims to provide access to the growing digital asset market.

data.title: Bybit introduces XAUT Flexible Easy Earn with boosted APR as gold hits $4,600 record. World's second-largest crypto exchange.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on January 14, 2026, Bybit, the second-largest cryptocurrency exchange globally with a high trading volume, has launched a new XAUT Flexible Easy Earn option as a part of Bybit Earn. This new offering includes an increased Annual Percentage Rate (APR) coinciding with a record-breaking gold price of $4,600. The initiative provides users with unique opportunities compared to traditional methods...

data.title: Global youth celebrate "Harmony for Shared Prosperity" event in Beijing hosted by Contemporary World magazine in Dec 2025.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, China Story reported that in December 2025, Contemporary World magazine organized the "Harmony for Shared Prosperity" event in Beijing, focusing on the upcoming generation. The event consisted of three parts, including an Exhibition Session and Remarks.

data.title: XRP up 3%, may see 70% rally. Ripple secures EU license for XRP usage in 27 countries. $95B payment volume, 75 licenses held.
data.description: XRP has seen a 3% increase in value within the past 24 hours, with indications pointing towards a potential 70% surge back to July's peak of $3.65. One of the reasons for this positive outlook is Ripple obtaining a Luxembourg EMI license for expanding operations in the European Union. This development grants Ripple approval to function as a licensed payment provider in all 27 EU countries. The "green light letter" issued by Luxembourg's financial regulator signifies a significant step for Ripple, allowing them to offer payment services utilizing XRP and stablecoins to banks and financial institutions across Europe. Following a similar approval in the UK, Ripple now has the green light to operate in two key European markets, having already processed over $95 billion in payment volume and holding more than 75 licenses globally. This EU approval unlocks a substantial new market for XRP-based transactions and services.
The Turkmen Foreign Ministry reported on the Iranian cargo ship Rona, which is in distress in the Caspian Sea

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs attract $754M with BTC surpassing $95K, highest flow since Oct. Experts remain cautious due to selective demand.
data.description: The Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) experienced their largest daily inflow since October as the price of Bitcoin reached $95,000. However, experts warn of potential selectivity in demand and advise caution amidst the high activity in the market.

data.title: Elon Musk announces Tesla's FSD tech will be subscription-based only. Read more on Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: On Wednesday, Elon Musk, the CEO of Tesla, revealed that the Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology will now exclusively be offered through a subscription model. This strategic shift indicates that customers can now access the FSD feature by subscribing to it. The move signifies a significant transition in Tesla's business model and is aimed at increasing accessibility to the FSD technology. Elon Musk's decision to introduce the subscription-based offering demonstrates a shift in the company's approach towards FSD and has sparked discussion within the investor community.

data.title: Honeywell's Quantinuum plans IPO joining the rising number of quantum computing stocks.
data.description: Honeywell has announced that its quantum computing division, Quantinuum, is seeking to go public through an IPO. This move contributes to the increasing presence of quantum computing companies within the stock market. The development underscores the rising interest in quantum technologies and the potential they hold for future advancements.

data.title: Capitolis achieves record-breaking performance in 2025 in portfolio optimization business across FX, Rates, and Credit products.
data.description: Capitolis, based in New York, has reported exceptional performance in 2025 in its portfolio optimization business, comprising FX, Rates, and Credit products. The company has achieved record-breaking results, indicating significant growth and success in its operations.

data.title: FreeWheel appoints Don Amboyer as GM of Strata, focusing on media management software, reporting to Mark McKee.
data.description: In New York today, FreeWheel, a prominent global technology platform for streaming advertising, named Don Amboyer as the new General Manager for Strata, the company's media management software. In this role, Amboyer will oversee Strata's operations, revenue, strategy, and product roadmap, reporting to FreeWheel General Manager Mark McKee. This move emphasizes FreeWheel's dedication to Strata by appointing a GM and solidifying it as a dedicated platform organization within the company.

data.title: Armis launches new partner program, Armis Select Partner Program, to expand global reach. Strengthen defenses with Armis Centrix™.
data.description: In San Francisco, Armis, a cybersecurity company, revealed the launch of the Armis Select Partner Program. This initiative signals a major advancement in Armis' dedication to its worldwide partner network. Through the program, Armis aims to expand the accessibility of Armis Centrix™, its Cyber Exposure Management Platform, to organizations on a global scale, enabling mutual customers to enhance their security operations and fortify defenses.

data.title: LibertyStream to move HQ to Texas, seeks shareholder approval in special meeting on March 31, 2026. $LIB.v #criticalminerals.
data.description: Dallas-based LibertyStream Infrastructure Partners Inc. (TSXV: LIB | OTCQB: VLTLF | FSE: I2D) has set March 31, 2026, as the date for a Special Meeting of Shareholders to discuss the proposed move of the company's headquarters to Dallas, Texas in the United States. The re-domiciliation plan aims to bring alignment to LibertyStream's corporate structure.

data.title: Global CEOs plan to increase enterprise AI investment despite concerns; new survey reports.
data.description: A recent white paper unveiled in Tokyo highlights the key challenges and opportunities facing CEOs in the realm of AI infrastructure optimization. The document outlines strategies for achieving efficient and sustainable AI frameworks.

data.title: OptConnect acquires DPL Wireless to enhance IoT innovations. The deal combines two tech companies for advanced connectivity solutions.
data.description: In Kaysville, Utah, OptConnect, a top company in managed wireless connectivity solutions, has recently acquired DPL Wireless, a Canadian company known for providing cellular connectivity and IoT device-intelligence solutions. Through this acquisition, OptConnect aims to enhance innovation in the IoT sector by introducing an advanced, AI-powered connectivity platform tailored for ATMs and other IoT applications. This strategic move brings together two reputable technology firms to deliver cutting-edge solutions.

data.title: QEP reports financial results for fiscal 2026: Nine-month & third-quarter results released, ending on Nov. 30, 2025.
data.description: In Boca Raton, Florida, on January 14, 2026, Q.E.P. CO., INC. (OTCQX: QEPC) disclosed its financial performance for the first nine months and third quarter of fiscal year 2026, concluding on November 30, 2025.
